name: Colm Bindstone
voicePatterns: short sentences and proverbs — 'A thread untended is a thread lost'
pointed silences that say more than words
refers to strangers as 'the wanderer' until they earn his name
folksy but with surprising depth — knows the weave as tradition, not theory
dialogueHooks: grumbling about the Wardweaver Guilds treating village traditions as superstition
sharing proverbs and folklore about the weave
guarding burial rites and the sanctity of the dead
reluctantly admitting what he knows about the Binding Song

always: burial rites and traditions of Thornwick going back generations
Morrith Hollowdark's teachings — the god of the frayed end
the Binding Song is performed at every ceremony of the Wheel of Binding
Maren Ashwick was the last proper Wardweaver in Thornwick
sometimes: the Fifth Verse was buried with Maren's grandmother Aldith
the specific burial traditions that protect the dead from the Fray
village folklore that predates the Wardweaver Guilds by centuries
rarely: he has performed the Binding Song solo for three years, improvising parts he doesn't know
the ward held despite his improvisation — which frightens him deeply
he suspects he may be unconsciously channeling real Threadcraft

morning.activity: morning prayers and tending the shrine to Morrith

afternoon.activity: walking the village, checking thread-cords on doors

evening.activity: nursing one ale, listening to village talk

night.activity: studying old ceremony texts by candlelight

backstory: Colm is the oldest person in Thornwick — 72 years of watching the village change, the young leave, and the old ways fade. He's not a Threadcraft practitioner by training, but as lay-priest of Morrith Hollowdark he has performed the village ceremonies for decades. When Maren grew too sick to lead three years ago, Colm took over the Binding Song solo. He doesn't know the full song. He's been improvising the parts he doesn't know — and the ward has held. This is either miraculous or terrifying, and Colm isn't sure which.
secret: Colm has been improvising portions of the Binding Song for three years and the ward held. Either he's unconsciously channeling real Threadcraft, or the weave is more forgiving than doctrine suggests. Both possibilities terrify him.
motivation: Preserve Thornwick's traditions and protect the dead from the Fray
tensions: keeper of tradition who may be accidentally breaking tradition's rules
distrusts outsiders but knows Thornwick needs outside help
stubborn about the old ways while secretly doubting they're enough

knownFacts: Bryn doesn't understand the old ways well enough
bryn_oakheart.history: They argue about everything but Colm trusts Bryn's heart. She's the best Thornwick has.

knownFacts: Sorrel has the talent to be the village's Wardweaver
sorrel_greenhand.history: Colm was like a grandfather to Sorrel. Her leaving broke something in him he won't admit.
